# Noise fixtures — license attribution

## musan_music_excerpt.wav

**Stand-in: synthetic music-like signal (no third-party source)**

Provenance: generated by `scripts/fetch-musan-music-fixture.sh`
            using ffmpeg's `aevalsrc` lavfi source.
Signal: 10 s three-tone harmonic chord (A4 440 Hz + E5 660 Hz + A5 880 Hz),
        16 kHz mono LE 16-bit PCM.
License: generated programmatically — no copyright, freely usable.

This synthetic clip exercises the harness pipeline against harmonic /
music-like content. It is a stand-in pending a real MUSAN clip.

### Upgrading to a real MUSAN clip

MUSAN corpus: https://www.openslr.org/17/
MUSAN license: Apache-2.0
Clip to use: musan/music/fma/music-fma-0000.wav (10 s trim)

To replace this synthetic stand-in with the real MUSAN clip, run:

```
FETCH_MUSAN=1 scripts/fetch-musan-music-fixture.sh
```

Note: this requires ~11 GB download. Once fetched, update this file
to reflect the real source:

```
Source: MUSAN corpus (https://www.openslr.org/17/), music/fma/music-fma-0000.wav
License: Apache-2.0
Provenance: extracted via scripts/fetch-musan-music-fixture.sh
            (10 s trim, 16 kHz mono LE 16-bit PCM via ffmpeg)
```
